root_text.sn56.59:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso himavato pabbatarājassa satta sāsapamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhipeyya.
root_text.sn56.59:1.3: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ—yā vā satta sāsapamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhittā, ayaṁ vā himavā pabbatarājā”ti?
root_text.sn56.59:1.4: “Etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ, yadidaṁ—himavā pabbatarājā; appamattikā satta sāsapamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhittā.
root_text.sn56.59:1.5: Saṅkhampi na upenti, upanidhampi na upenti, kalabhāgampi na upenti himavantaṁ pabbatarājānaṁ upanidhāya satta sāsapamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhittā”ti.

translation_text.sn56.59:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose a person was to place seven pebbles the size of mustard seeds on the Himalayas, the king of mountains.
translation_text.sn56.59:1.3: Which is more: the seven pebbles the size of mustard seeds, or the Himalayas, the king of mountains?”
translation_text.sn56.59:1.4: “Sir, the Himalayas, the king of mountains, is certainly more. The seven pebbles the size of mustard seeds are tiny.
translation_text.sn56.59:1.5: Compared to the Himalayas, they don’t count, there’s no comparison, they’re not worth a fraction.”
translation_text.sn56.59:1.6: “In the same way, for a noble disciple …
translation_text.sn56.59:1.7: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
